payload.claim_text: Signal timing changes: northbound-southbound green time added at Linden Boulevard; coordination of signals approaching Eastern Parkway improved.

Select Bus Service (SBS) is a joint program between MTA New York City Transit (NYCT) and the New York City Department of Transportation (NYC DOT), which aims to improve transit service through the application of Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) principles to New York City's busiest bus routes.
Minimize impact to vehicular traffic flow on project corridors, and more efficiently utilize street space for motorists, transit riders, and pedestrians
NYC DOT is planning to install at least 18 additional real-time bus arrival signs on Nostrand, Rogers, and Bedford Avenues in 2016.
NYCT interviewed 1,186 shoppers on Nostrand Avenue in the summer of 2011 near Glenwood Road, Church Avenue, Empire Boulevard, and Fulton Street.
The project plan qualified as a Type II action under local (CEQR) guidelines and as a Categorical Exclusion under federal (NEPA) guidelines.

The B44 was the fifth and final Phase I SBS route to be implemented, the 6th SBS route overall, and is now one of nine SBS routes in the city. The first four Phase I corridors (Bx12, M15, M34, & S79) were implemented between 2008 and 2012.
Williamsburg Bridge Bus Plaza reconstruction requested by Brooklyn Community Board 1, which cited the inconvenience and unattractiveness of the previous facility.
Increase street safety across all modes on the project corridors

The B44 is the 3rd highest ridership bus route in Brooklyn, and the 6th highest in all of New York City. Approximately 60% of residents within 1/4 mile of the service commute by transit, and more than half of households do not own a car.
Introduce a new, high performance transit option for NYC by improving the speed, reliability, and attractiveness of the B44
For the November 2013 launch, one real-time information sign was installed at the Church Avenue stop on Nostrand Avenue.
67% expressed interest in a delivery zone either directly in front of their store, across the street, or around the corner. 88% of deliveries take less than 30 minutes to complete.
A 10 AM – 4 PM window for deliveries, truck loading, or customer parking. Delivery window typically 10 AM – 12 PM on east side, 12 – 2 PM on west side.
Provide measurable benefits to existing B44 users and attract new riders

Speeding prevalent in AM and PM peaks on Rogers Avenue. DOT restoring PM parking and some AM parking on Rogers Avenue between Beverly Road and Maple Street to narrow roadway and lessen speeding opportunities.

192 storefront businesses surveyed along Nostrand Avenue between Eastern Parkway and Linden Boulevard. 149 additional businesses surveyed by mail on Nostrand, Rogers, and Bedford Avenues between Flushing Avenue and Avenue I. Summer 2011.

payload.publisher: New York City Transit / NYC DOT
payload.source_title: B44 Select Bus Service Nostrand Avenue Progress Report

payload.description: 9.6 miles of bus lanes implemented along B44 SRS counting both directions, covering just over half the route. Offset bus lane configuration on Nostrand Avenue; curbside bus lanes where space constrained.
Northbound B44 SBS rerouted from New York Avenue to Rogers Avenue, decreasing northbound route from 9.9 to 9.5 miles. B44 Local maintained on New York Avenue.
15 bus bulbs constructed — eight on Nostrand Avenue and seven on Rogers and Bedford Avenues. All north of Flatbush Avenue. High curb nearly level with bus floor.
Real-time bus arrival signs. One installed at Church Avenue for launch. At least 18 more planned on Nostrand, Rogers, and Bedford Avenues in 2016.
62-foot long, 3-door articulated buses replaced 40-foot, 2-door buses for B44 SBS — first use of such buses in Brooklyn. 40-foot, 2-door buses continue on B44 Local.
Off-board fare collection with MetroCard and coin machines at every bus stop. Roving fare inspectors check tickets on board.
5 neckdowns (curb extensions at intersection crosswalks) installed at locations identified by school safety studies
